# Prior predictive checks

Before fitting, ask: **do my priors imply sensible data?** A prior predictive
check answers this by simulating fake datasets from the priors *alone* — no
observed data involved.

## How it works

1. Draw parameter values from their priors.
2. Push them through the model to simulate a dataset.
3. Repeat many times and look at the spread of simulated data.

If your "fish lengths" range from $-400$ to $600$ mm, your priors are absurd —
they put weight on impossible values. Tighten them.

## In FANGS

Use the **Prior Check** tab. It runs the model forward from the priors with the
likelihood switched off, so you see exactly what your priors predict before the
data has any say.

This is the natural companion to the σ-prior work you just did: a vague
`dnorm(0, 1000)` on an intercept looks harmless until you see the data it
implies.

## Your task

Run a prior check on your fish-length model. Then answer the question below about
interpreting an obviously-too-vague prior.
